info.name: Caflou
info.description:
Welcome to the documentation for Caflou API v1. Documentation is continuously updated, changes may occur.

On all LIST requests, you can also pass per parameter so you can list more than 20 records (which is the default). The maximum value is 1000 but use it wisely, please. Your access can be limited due to overload.

You can also pass the filter as a parameter with its values related to the current bject type. Available filter values are not documented yet but you can read them from the web UI. Example:

{
  "filter": {
    "search": "Find this name",
    "start_date": "2022-12-31",
    "company_ids": "1,2"  
  }
}

item.name: Authentication

header.description: hash - base64 encoded email:password

request.description:
https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basic_access_authentication

Example of creating hash value in JavaScript:

const email = 'your@email.com'
const password = 'yourPassword'
const hash = btoa(`${email}:${password}`)

description.content: If you have enabled 2-factor authentication provide one time password (the 6 digits code).

header.description: base64 hash of email:password

query.description: If you have enabled 2-factor authentication provide one time password (the 6 digits code).

header.name: Cache-Control
header.description: Tells all caching mechanisms from server to client whether they may cache this object. It is measured in seconds

header.name: Connection
header.description: Options that are desired for the connection

header.name: Content-Encoding
header.description: The type of encoding used on the data.

header.name: Content-Type
header.description: The mime type of this content

header.name: Date
header.description: The date and time that the message was sent

header.name: Front-End-Https
header.description: Custom header

header.name: Server
header.description: A name for the server

header.name: Strict-Transport-Security
header.description: A HSTS Policy informing the HTTP client how long to cache the HTTPS only policy and whether this applies to subdomains.

header.name: Transfer-Encoding
header.description: The form of encoding used to safely transfer the entity to the user. Currently defined methods are: chunked, compress, deflate, gzip, identity.

header.name: X-Content-Type-Options
header.description: The only defined value, "nosniff", prevents Internet Explorer from MIME-sniffing a response away from the declared content-type

header.name: X-Frame-Options
header.description: Clickjacking protection: "deny" - no rendering within a frame, "sameorigin" - no rendering if origin mismatch

header.name: X-Request-Id
header.description: Custom header

header.name: X-Runtime
header.description: Custom header

header.name: X-UA-Compatible
header.description: Custom header

header.name: X-XSS-Protection
header.description: Cross-site scripting (XSS) filter

item.description:
On each further API request you will need an valid access_token. To gain an access_token POST credentials of the user (email and password) as 'Basic Auth' - https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Basic_access_authentication

Access tokens expire 1 week after creation.

item.name: Accounts

request.description: Get list of the accounts user has access to.

item.description: The most of API requests are in scope of the Account. For this purpose you need {account_id} parameter which is part of the URL path.

request.description: Get list of the users authenticated user can see. Note that there are fictive users also.

response.name: List

request.description:
Create single file upload with multipart form request.

Create single file upload.

File can be uploaded in 3 ways:

  1. by providng multipart form attachmetfile

  2. by providing url

  3. by providing base64 with filename

description string
record_reason string
privacy json

If you want to update custom column with name "my_attr" you have to patch object with prefixed attribute name "custom_column_my_attr".

item.name: Update custom column in nested objects

request.description: If you want to update custom column with name "my_attr" in nested object, you have to patch parent object. In nested attributes just paste whole "custom_columns" attribute in JSON structure to update ALL custom columns attributes in the nested object. To avodi deleting another custom columns, you have to send all unchanged custom columns of nested object too.
